title: Emilia longiramea
taxonomy: Emilia longiramea belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. This family consists of over 23,000 species, many of which are economically important. The genus Emilia is a small group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. As a member of the genus Emilia, E. longiramea is a herbaceous plant that produces small, daisy-like flowers. The classification of E. longiramea places it within the tribe Senecioneae, which is a large group of plants that are often characterized by their daisy-like flowers.

Emilia coccinea, also known as Red Emilia or Fire Emilia, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a tropical plant native to Africa and Asia, often used in traditional medicine.

The health benefits of Emilia coccinea include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections.

The bioactive constituents of Emilia coccinea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Emilia coccinea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
